results.title: More Winners : His master's ghost (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Steve Steve Jodrell, Margot McDonald and Australian Children's Television Foundation
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series ‘More Winners’.
A group of children are brought together to holiday at a music camp. set at an old estate, rumoured to have its own live-in ghost. One of the children, Flea, becomes obsessed with tracking down the phantom. Meanwhile Jason is attempting to get his Concerto for Rock Group and Orchestra approved of. During all this, strange things begin to happen whenever the estate’s mysterious care-taker is around.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Steve Steve Jodrell, Margot McDonald and Australian Children’s Television Foundation

results.title: More Winners : Boy Soldiers (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Mark Joffe, Margot McDonald and Australian Children's Television Foundation
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series ‘More Winners’.
In 1910, the Australian Government passed a law requiring all boys aged between 14 and 17 years to register for compulsory military training. Between 1910 and 1915, more than 30,000 boys were prosecuted for failing to obey this law. This story tells of one such boy, Will Barnes, a 14 year old conscientious objector who refuses to register. After prosecution Will is sentenced to 3 months training at Fort Queenscliff. At the fort, Will refuses to wear a uniform or be involved in any type of military training. Will meets a lot of opposition, but also wins his share of admirers for sticking to his beliefs.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Mark Joffe, Margot McDonald and Australian Children’s Television Foundation

results.title: Master Photographers Series
results.brief_description: A six part series on the men who started their careers early this century, and who are considered pioneers in their specific fields of photography.

results.title: Ansel Adams
results.brief_description:
Episode number 6 of Series “Master Photographers Series”.
America’s most distinguished landscape photographer, Ansel Adams also has a reputation as one of the world’s greatest printers of photographs.

group_siblings.title: Alfred Eisenstaedt
group_siblings.creator_credit: Peter Adam and British Broadcasting Corporation

results.title: Andre Kertesz
results.brief_description:
Episode number 5 of Series “Master Photographers Series”.
Hungarian born Kertesz is considered the poet amongst photographers. In this film Kertesz talks explicitly about the influences on his work, his early life in Hungary, Paris in the 20’s, his colleagues and his friends, Chagall, Mondrian and Colette, and his bitter and long battle for acceptance in the United States.

results.title: Andreas Feininger
results.brief_description:
Episode number 3 of Series “Master Photographers Series”.
A fascinating look at the technical possibilities of creative photography and at Feininger’s firm belief that the camera can enrich people’s way of seeing.

results.title: Bill Brandt
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Master Photographers Series”.
In this rare insight, Brandt shows some of his favourite photographs among them pictures of Paris in the 1920s, his compassionate scenes of Britain in the Depression, his Nudes and many distinguished portraits.

results.title: Alfred Eisenstaedt
results.creator_credit: Peter Adam and British Broadcasting Corporation
results.brief_description:
Episode number 1 of Series “Master Photographers Series”.
Looks at the life and work of former ‘Life’ magazine photographer Alfred Eisenstaedt. Concentrates on portraits of celebrities from 1929 to the present.
